nonNegotiables: Support only audit value label for this story and reject unsupported values with a validation error.
Treat missing, null, and empty audit lists identically and make no extra GitLab calls in that case.
Run base search first, then fetch label events per returned issue in parallel via AsyncComposer.
Extend orchestration Issue with optional changeSets while keeping label-event models reused and transport-only DTO decisions at the REST boundary.
Enforce default perPage 20 and maximum perPage 40 with full failure if any enrichment call fails.

scope.purpose: Extend only POST /api/issues/search so clients can request label-audit enrichment while create, delete, and get-single contracts stay unchanged.
inScope: Add filters.audit to the search request and map it to an orchestration enum list ready for future audit types.
Extend orchestration Issue with nullable changeSets reused from the existing label-event models.
Introduce a search-only response item DTO so search maps change.field to the literal transport value label without changing create/delete or get-single DTO contracts.
Run label-event enrichment after search returns by using AsyncComposer for fail-fast per-issue parallel fan-out.
Lock the shipped page-size contract to default 20 and maximum 40 at startup and in request handling.
Update search-focused unit, integration, component, HTTP, and Karate coverage for valid, invalid, and full-failure paths.
outOfScope: Changing the existing get-single label-event transport contract.
Partial-success enrichment or background processing when one label-event call fails.
New shared infrastructure extraction or new capability packages.
constraints: Follow constitution boundary rules: orchestration decides composition, integration stays single-call-per-method, and REST DTOs remain transport-only.

Reuse AsyncComposer, IssuesPort.getLabelEvents(long issueId), GitLabIssuesAdapter, GitLabLabelEventMapper, ChangeSet, Change, LabelChangeSet, LabelChange, ChangedBy, and ChangeField rather than duplicating orchestration audit models.
Keep IssueDto unchanged so create and delete transport contracts remain byte-for-byte compatible with current behavior.
Keep the change inside the existing issues capability; no new ArchUnit rule is needed because packages and dependencies stay within already enforced boundaries.
Documentation and tests must stay aligned with the official GitLab endpoints GET /projects/:id/issues and GET /projects/:id/issues/:issue_iid/resource_label_events.

validationBoundaryDecision.decision: REST request models own audit sanitization and supported-value validation, configuration owns fixed 20/40 startup validation, IssuesResponseMapper owns the search-only literal label transport mapping, and IssuesService owns downstream branching plus full-failure enforcement.
details: IssueFiltersRequest drops null audit entries before validation so ["label", null] becomes ["label"].
IssueFiltersRequest rejects every non-null audit value other than label before orchestration mapping runs.
IssuesRequestMapper defaults missing page to 1 and missing perPage to the fixed contract value 20.
IssuesApiProperties fails startup unless defaultPageSize == 20 and maxPageSize == 40, preventing profile drift.
IssuesResponseMapper maps orchestration ChangeField.LABEL to the literal string label for search responses instead of relying on enum serialization behavior.
IssuesService decides whether label-event calls run and propagates the first enrichment failure as a full request failure.

compositionStrategy.sequentialDependency: Label-event calls need project issue IIDs from the initial search response, so search executes first.
compositionStrategy.parallelWork: After search completes and auditTypes contains LABEL, one AsyncComposer task per returned Issue.issueId() calls IssuesPort.getLabelEvents(issueId).
compositionStrategy.failureMode: AsyncComposer joinFailFast cancels siblings on first failure and IssuesService returns no partial IssuePage.

assumptions: GitLab project issue search uses GET /projects/:id/issues and the existing adapter already maps pagination headers correctly.
GitLab resource label events use GET /projects/:id/issues/:issue_iid/resource_label_events, so enrichment must call the port with Issue.issueId() rather than Issue.id().
The existing get-single endpoint keeps its current transport contract; this feature only introduces the literal search transport value label at the search DTO boundary.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Accept filters.audit, drop null entries, reject unsupported values, default page to 1 and perPage to 20, and map distinct audit strings to IssueAuditType.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Input validation and transport-to-orchestration mapping belong at the REST boundary.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Lock startup config to 20/40 and expose a search-only response item DTO whose change.field is mapped as the literal string label while create/delete keep IssueDto unchanged.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Configuration validation belongs in config and transport adaptations belong in the REST layer without leaking into orchestration models.
outputs: Search-only transport contract isolated from other endpoints

implementationFlow.responsibility: Run the existing search first, reject perPage values above 40, and return the base IssuePage unchanged when auditTypes is empty or no issues are returned.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Only orchestration may decide whether additional downstream calls are necessary.

implementationFlow.responsibility: For auditTypes containing LABEL, fan out one getLabelEvents(issue.issueId()) call per returned issue, preserve result order, and rebuild each Issue with nullable-or-present changeSets.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Composition across independent port calls belongs in orchestration and must execute in parallel when the only dependency is the base search result.
outputs: Enriched IssuePage with reused orchestration changeSets

implementationFlow.responsibility: Verify no-audit bypass, audit success, unsupported audit validation, perPage 41 rejection, and full-failure semantics while updating search HTTP and Karate artifacts so POST /api/issues/search asserts label and get-single keeps LABEL.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Changed API behavior must be provable with automated tests and aligned documentation.

contractExamples.label: Search success item with label audit changeSets
Search success item without audit omits changeSets
Search validation failure for unsupported audit

body.message: GitLab get label events operation failed

validationRules.rule: IssueFiltersRequest.audit may be absent, null, empty, or contain null entries; null entries are removed before validation and the resulting list stays immutable.
validationRules.reason: The request contract explicitly treats missing, null, empty, and null-only audit values as equivalent no-audit input.

validationRules.rule: Every non-null audit value must equal label; unsupported values fail with a 400 validation response before IssuesService executes.
validationRules.reason: Supported values are a boundary concern and require explicit client feedback.

validationRules.rule: IssuesRequestMapper defaults page to 1, perPage to 20, and maps audit strings to a distinct immutable List<IssueAuditType> in request order.
validationRules.reason: Transport defaulting and normalization belong at the REST-to-orchestration boundary.

validationRules.rule: IssuesApiProperties must fail startup unless defaultPageSize == 20 and maxPageSize == 40.
validationRules.reason: The locked API contract cannot drift through profile or environment overrides.

validationRules.rule: IssuesService must make zero label-event calls when auditTypes is empty and must reject perPage values above 40.
validationRules.reason: The orchestration use case owns downstream branching and protects non-REST callers from oversized requests.

validationRules.rule: If any label-event call fails, the entire search request fails and no partially enriched IssuePage is returned.
validationRules.reason: The locked request explicitly rejects partial success for this story.

designDecisions.title: Keep the change inside the existing issues capability
designDecisions.decision: Modify only the existing search path, request mapper, shared Issue model, search response transport, config, and search-focused tests inside the issues capability.
designDecisions.rationale: The story is a direct extension of POST /api/issues/search and existing label-event plumbing already lives in the same capability.
alternatives: Introduce a new capability package for audit history

designDecisions.title: Search first, then enrich in parallel
designDecisions.decision: Execute issuesPort.getIssues(query) first, then submit one issuesPort.getLabelEvents(issue.issueId()) task per returned issue through AsyncComposer and rebuild the page after all tasks succeed.
designDecisions.rationale: Label-event calls depend on issue IIDs from the base search result, but once those IDs exist the calls are independent and should run in parallel for latency control.
alternatives: Sequentially fetch label events for each issue
Teach the integration adapter to compose search and label events internally

designDecisions.title: Reuse orchestration Issue plus nullable changeSets
designDecisions.decision: Extend orchestration Issue with @Nullable List<ChangeSet> changeSets and keep it null when audit is not requested; IssuePage continues to carry List<Issue>.
designDecisions.rationale: The locked request explicitly requires extending the shared orchestration Issue model instead of creating a parallel enriched output model.
alternatives: Introduce a search-only orchestration issue type
Keep changeSets outside Issue in a side-map

designDecisions.title: Isolate the search transport contract from other endpoints
designDecisions.decision: Keep IssueDto unchanged for create/delete and switch search responses to SearchIssueDto, which owns optional changeSets and carries change.field as a plain transport string fixed to label by IssuesResponseMapper.
designDecisions.rationale: This preserves the locked search response example, avoids leaking search-only null handling or wire values into unrelated endpoint contracts, and removes serializer-dependent ambiguity that already caused the implementation and Karate smoke paths to diverge.
alternatives: Add changeSets directly to IssueDto with property-level null omission
Change the existing get-single DTO hierarchy to lowercase wire values
Keep a search-only enum and rely on Jackson enum serialization annotations

designDecisions.title: Treat 20 and 40 as fixed shipped API contract values
designDecisions.decision: Set application defaults to default-page-size 20 and max-page-size 40 and make IssuesApiProperties reject any startup configuration that does not exactly match those two values.
designDecisions.rationale: The locked client contract must remain stable across profiles and deployments, so both the default and the maximum need hard enforcement.
alternatives: Hardcode 20 only in the mapper and leave configuration drift possible
Allow wider maxPageSize through environment overrides

models.purpose: Extensible orchestration discriminator for requested audit enrichments; currently only LABEL is supported.
models.placementJustification: Audit type selection is business meaning for the search use case, so the enum belongs in orchestration rather than REST transport.

models.purpose: Search use-case input carrying pagination, existing filters, and requested audit types.
models.placementJustification: IssueQuery is the provider-agnostic orchestration input for issue search and must own normalized audit intent.

models.purpose: Unified orchestration output for search and create, now optionally carrying changeSets when search audit enrichment runs.
models.placementJustification: The locked request explicitly requires extending the shared orchestration Issue model instead of introducing a parallel enriched output type.

models.purpose: Transport carrier for search filters, including audit values that are sanitized and validated before mapping to orchestration enums.
models.placementJustification: This is an HTTP boundary DTO and should carry raw client values plus declarative validation, not business behavior.

fields.name: audit
fields.validation: sanitize nulls first, then each non-null element must equal label
fields.defensiveCopy: null becomes List.of(); otherwise List.copyOf(filtered values)

models.purpose: Search-only issue response payload that keeps create/delete transport unchanged while optionally carrying audit changeSets.
models.placementJustification: A dedicated REST DTO is allowed by the locked request and is the safest way to isolate search-only transport concerns from other endpoints.

fields.name: changeSets
fields.serialization: omit when null via property-level NON_NULL inclusion

models.purpose: Search transport change-set payload for label audit entries.
models.placementJustification: This structure is transport-only and exists to keep the search wire contract independent from get-single DTO reuse.

models.purpose: Search transport actor payload for changeSets.
models.placementJustification: Actor details are reused from orchestration but adapted as a REST transport record inside the search-only DTO file.

models.purpose: Search transport payload for the changed label inside each search changeSet.
models.placementJustification: This REST record exists only to map shared orchestration label changes to the locked lowercase search contract without introducing a second enum contract at the HTTP boundary.

models.purpose: Reusable issue-history contract for changeSet payloads.
models.placementJustification: This interface already carries provider-agnostic audit semantics in orchestration and is the mandated reuse target.

models.purpose: Reusable issue-history contract for the changed field payload.
models.placementJustification: The orchestration layer owns the business meaning of what changed, so this shared interface stays unchanged.

models.purpose: Label-event implementation of ChangeSet reused by get-single and search audit responses.
models.placementJustification: The label-event mapping already exists in orchestration and must be reused rather than duplicated.

models.purpose: Label-specific Change implementation reused by get-single and search audit responses.
models.placementJustification: The change payload is already an orchestration model and must remain the single source of label-change semantics.

models.purpose: Reusable actor metadata for issue audit entries.
models.placementJustification: Actor metadata is already part of the shared orchestration audit model and should remain unchanged.

models.purpose: Shared orchestration discriminator for audit change types.
models.placementJustification: The business-level change-field enum stays in orchestration and must not be repurposed for endpoint-specific wire formatting.

classes.role: New orchestration audit enum with wire-value lookup.
Search input model gains immutable auditTypes.
Shared issue output gains nullable changeSets with null-preserving defensive copy.
Search filter DTO gains audit transport field, null sanitization, and supported-value validation.
Maps audit strings to IssueAuditType values and defaults perPage to 20.
Search orchestration branches on auditTypes, runs fail-fast parallel enrichment, and preserves no-audit behavior.
Search-only response DTO file containing the item payload, nested change-set records, and the plain-string search change.field contract.
Search wrapper switches items from IssueDto to SearchIssueDto.
Startup validation enforces defaultPageSize 20 and maxPageSize 40 exactly.
Default issues API configuration moves to 20/40 to match the locked contract.
Maps IssuePage items to SearchIssueDto and converts ChangeField.LABEL to the literal search transport string label.
Unit coverage for no-audit bypass, parallel enrichment, order preservation, and full-failure propagation.
Unit coverage for audit null sanitization, immutability, null-only audit, and unsupported values.
Unit coverage for default perPage 20, audit enum mapping, and duplicate label audit normalization.
Unit coverage for SearchIssuesResponse wrapper with SearchIssueDto items and null changeSets omission serialization.
Unit coverage for lowercase label transport mapping and search item omission when changeSets is null.
Unit coverage proving startup config rejects anything other than 20/40.
Integration coverage for audit validation, fixed default perPage 20, lowercase search response field, and perPage 41 rejection.
Component coverage for search audit success, no-audit no-call behavior, and full request failure when one label-event call fails.
HTTP examples add search-with-audit, empty-audit, unsupported-audit, and perPage 41 requests with the search-only response shape.
Karate smoke scenarios cover no-audit search, label-audit search, and validation failure for unsupported audit or perPage > 40.
Component stub support exposes search responses for no-audit and audit-enriched pages with perPage 20 defaults.
Component stub support adds multi-issue label-event success and one-call failure scenarios for fail-fast verification.

slices.title: Boundary Contract Isolation
slices.goal: Add audit input, fixed 20/40 boundary enforcement, and a search-only transport contract that leaves create/delete unchanged.
implementationTasks: Add IssueAuditType and extend IssueQuery with immutable auditTypes.
Extend IssueFiltersRequest and IssuesRequestMapper to sanitize audit input, reject unsupported values, and default perPage to 20.
Tighten IssuesApiProperties and application.yml so startup rejects any page-size contract drift away from 20/40.
Introduce SearchIssueDto and update SearchIssuesResponse plus IssuesResponseMapper so search uses a dedicated DTO with nullable changeSets and change.field fixed to the literal string label.
Add focused unit tests for request DTO validation, mapper defaulting, search DTO serialization, response mapping, and property validation.

integration: IssuesControllerIT validation and response-shape assertions for the search endpoint

info: None - this slice changes validation and transport mapping only; no new log lines are required.

doneWhen: Search request binding accepts absent/null/empty audit and rejects unsupported values before service execution.
SearchIssueDto exposes change.field as the literal lowercase label value and omits changeSets when null.
IssueDto remains unchanged so create/delete transport contract does not gain search-only fields.
Startup validation fails for any defaultPageSize/maxPageSize pair other than 20/40.

slices.title: Parallel Audit Enrichment
slices.goal: Reuse the shared Issue model and existing label-event models to enrich search results only when auditTypes contains LABEL, with fail-fast parallel execution and search-focused verification.
implementationTasks: Extend Issue with nullable changeSets and update IssuesService.getIssues to branch on auditTypes after validating perPage.
Return the base IssuePage unchanged when auditTypes is empty or the search result page is empty.
When auditTypes contains LABEL, call getLabelEvents(issue.issueId()) in parallel via AsyncComposer, preserve item order, and rebuild each Issue with reused orchestration changeSets.
Propagate the first enrichment failure as a full search failure without partial results or fallback payloads.

Expand integration, component, HTTP, and Karate search verification for no-audit, label-audit, unsupported-audit, perPage 41, and enrichment-failure paths, with explicit assertions that search returns label while get-single remains LABEL.

integration: IssuesControllerIT search success, validation failure, and error propagation assertions

info: IssuesService.getIssues logs page, perPage, auditTypes, itemCount, enrichmentApplied, and durationMs for enriched requests.
error: None - downstream failures continue through the existing exception mapping path without duplicate error logging.

doneWhen: Search executes before any label-event fetch and no label-event calls run when auditTypes is empty.
Label-event enrichment runs one call per returned issue IID through AsyncComposer and preserves search item order.
Any enrichment failure aborts the whole request and returns the sanitized integration error contract.
Search-focused integration, component, HTTP, and Karate artifacts prove valid, invalid, and failure paths, and no search artifact keeps the stale uppercase LABEL expectation.

checks: Unit coverage for audit sanitization, mapper defaulting, explicit search change.field mapping to label, response-shape omission behavior, and property validation.
Integration coverage for unsupported audit rejection, default perPage 20, and POST /api/issues/search returning change.field == label.
slices.evidence: IssueFiltersRequestTest, IssuesRequestMapperTest, SearchIssuesResponseTest, IssuesResponseMapperTest, IssuesApiPropertiesTest, and IssuesControllerIT

checks: Unit coverage for no-audit bypass, parallel enrichment, order preservation, and full-failure propagation.
Integration coverage for search success, perPage 41 rejection, and sanitized failure mapping.
Component coverage for multi-issue audit enrichment, no-audit no-call behavior, one-label-event-call failure aborting the whole request, and search change.field == label on the running app.
Karate smoke coverage for no-audit search, label-audit search with change.field == label, and invalid search requests, while get-single smoke keeps its existing LABEL assertion.
